site.name: GuedesLab
site.description: GuedesLab is coordinated by Prof. Dr. Herbert Leonel de Matos Guedes, head of the Libtec and LIC laboratories, affiliated with the Institute of Microbiology Paulo de Goés at UFRJ and the Oswaldo Cruz Institute (FIOCRUZ).

lab.title: GuedesLab
lab.subtitle: Laboratory of ImunoBiotechnology (Libtec) and Clinical Immunology Laboratory (LIC)
lab.description: GuedesLab is coordinated by Prof. Dr. Herbert Leonel de Matos Guedes, head of the Libtec and LIC laboratories, affiliated with the Institute of Microbiology Paulo de Goés at UFRJ and the Oswaldo Cruz Institute (FIOCRUZ). Our research focuses on immunology of leishmaniasis, vaccinology, cell therapy and immunotherapy, pursuing new therapeutic and vaccine strategies against parasitic and fungal diseases.

hero.eyebrow: UFRJ · FIOCRUZ / IOC · INCT Mucosa e Pele · Institute of Microbiology Paulo de Goés
hero.title: GuedesLab
hero.body_1: Coordinated by Prof. Dr. Herbert Leonel de Matos Guedes, GuedesLab brings together two complementary laboratories — Libtec (Laboratory of ImunoBiotechnology) and LIC (Clinical Immunology Laboratory) — focusing on immunology of leishmaniasis, vaccinology, cell therapy and immunotherapy.
hero.body_2: Projects are affiliated with the Institute of Microbiology Paulo de Goés at UFRJ and the Oswaldo Cruz Institute (IOC / FIOCRUZ). The institutional partnership includes Prof. Dr. Alessandra Marcia da Fonseca Martins (UNIRIO).

section.description: Professors, postdocs, PhD students, master's students and undergraduates in the Libtec and LIC laboratories.

patricia.group_title: Núcleo de Pesquisa em Leishmanioses — UFF
patricia.description: Prof. Dr. Patrícia de Almeida Machado is an Adjunct Professor in the Department of Pathology at the Universidade Federal Fluminense (UFF) and conducts research in parasitology and molecular biology of protozoa, with emphasis on the prospection of new therapeutic targets and bioactive molecules for the treatment of leishmaniases. From 2017 to 2024, she completed a postdoctoral fellowship under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Herbert Leonel de Matos Guedes, during which she developed studies on the evaluation of compounds with antileishmanial potential, particularly those targeting the inhibition of parasitic proteases, seeking alternatives or complementary strategies to the chemotherapy currently available for leishmaniases. She currently maintains a scientific collaboration with GuedesLab.

imunirio.title: ImUNIRIO — University Extension
imunirio.description: ImUNIRIO is an extension project aimed at scientific education, immunology outreach, and health promotion. Our goal is to bring the university closer to the community through educational actions on vaccines, disease prevention, immunotherapy, and science, using accessible and evidence-based language. We also seek to combat fake news and demystify scientific topics, preparing students for scientific communication and social impact.

section.description: Two complementary units — Libtec and LIC — focusing on immunology, vaccinology, cell therapy and parasitic biology.

imunologia-basica.title: Basic Immunology
imunologia-basica.description: Fundamental immunological concepts and mechanisms in the context of infection and vaccination.

vacinologia.title: Vaccinology
vacinologia.description: Development, production and testing of new vaccine candidates against parasitic, fungal and viral infections, including intranasal and second-generation vaccines.

biologia-celular-molecular.title: Cell and Molecular Biology
biologia-celular-molecular.description: Study of cell structures, functions and operations at microscopic and molecular level, focusing on parasites and resistance mechanisms.

terapia-celular-imunoterapia.title: Cell Therapy and Immunotherapy
terapia-celular-imunoterapia.description: Use of extracellular vesicles, mesenchymal stromal cells and immunotherapeutic compounds to control leishmaniasis and other diseases.

neuroimunologia.title: Neuroimmunology
neuroimunologia.description: Evaluation of nociception, pain and behavior in Leishmania amazonensis infection and neuro-immune interaction.

microbiologia.title: Microbiology and Mycology
microbiologia.description: Studies of microbiota influence on the course of Leishmania infections and immune response to fungal infections such as Sporothrix and Cryptococcus.

farmacologia.title: Pharmacology (Chemotherapy)
farmacologia.description: Study of the efficacy of chemical substances and drugs against Leishmania species parasites, including protease inhibitors and antiparasitics.

section.description: Articles published in peer-reviewed international journals.
